doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this cough for about two weeks now. It’s mostly dry, but sometimes I cough up, like, whitish or clear phlegm. I’m kinda worried because it’s been persistent, you know?

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the cough starting?
patient_turn: I think it started about two weeks ago, um, maybe a little more? It just kind of came out of nowhere, and it hasn’t really gone away since then.

doctor_turn: Has the cough changed at all since it first started?
patient_turn: Uh, not really, I guess. It’s mostly been the same, just dry with some clear phlegm occasionally. Sometimes it makes me wheeze a bit, and I get this dull chest pain after I cough a lot, but, um, that’s about it.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the chest pain when it occurs?
patient_turn: It’s, um, like a dull pain in my chest, mostly in the middle, I think? It happens after I cough for a while, you know, like it’s just sore from all the coughing. It’s not super intense, but it’s definitely there.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, like your arms, neck, or back?
patient_turn: Oh, you know, I’ve been thinking about getting a new plant for my apartment. I really want something that’s easy to take care of.

doctor_turn: What does the chest pain feel like when it happens? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Um, it’s definitely more of a dull pain, I think. It’s not sharp or anything, just, like, uncomfortable after I’ve been coughing a lot. It kind of feels like my chest is just sore, you know?

doctor_turn: What makes the pain feel better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Well, um, I’ve noticed that it gets worse when I cough a lot, obviously. But, like, resting a bit seems to help, I guess? I try to take it easy after I’ve been coughing, and that makes it feel a little better.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the chest pain?
patient_turn: You know, I’ve been meaning to ask about getting a new phone. Mine is kind of old and slow now.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where the pain is located?
patient_turn: Uh, yeah, it’s mostly in the middle of my chest, I think. It’s not on one side or the other, just kind of right in the center. It’s, um, not super intense, but it’s definitely noticeable after I cough a lot.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, like your arms, neck, or back?
patient_turn: No, um, it doesn’t really spread anywhere else. It’s just in my chest, you know? I haven’t felt it in my arms or neck or anything like that, just, um, right there in the middle.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like when it occurs?
patient_turn: It’s, um, mostly a dull ache, I guess. It’s not sharp or anything, just kind of uncomfortable after I’ve been coughing a lot. It feels like my chest is sore, you know?

doctor_turn: What makes the pain feel better or worse for you?
patient_turn: I guess the pain is, like, kind of there sometimes, but I’m not really sure how to explain it. It might get worse when I cough, or maybe not, I can’t really remember.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any other symptoms along with the chest pain, like breathlessness or dizziness?
patient_turn: Um, no, I haven’t noticed anything like that. It’s just the cough and the chest pain, really. I mean, I don’t feel breathless or dizzy or anything, so that’s a relief, I guess.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me more about your daily habits, like smoking, drinking, or exercise?
patient_turn: Oh, um, I smoke occasionally, like about once a month now. I used to smoke more, but I’ve cut back a lot. I also have a glass of wine, like, every night, but I don’t really drink much beyond that. As for exercise, I try to stay active, but I haven’t been super consistent lately, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you had any past medical problems or are you currently taking any medications?
patient_turn: Uh, my past medical history is pretty unremarkable, honestly. The only thing I had was my wisdom teeth removed about 8 or 9 years ago. I’m currently on a birth control pill that I started three years ago, and I haven’t had any side effects from it.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any green or bloody sputum when you cough?
patient_turn: No, um, I haven’t noticed any green or bloody sputum at all. It’s mostly just clear or whitish when I do cough something up. So, that’s a relief, I guess.

doctor_turn: What happened during your last cough episode 3-4 years ago?
patient_turn: Oh, um, I remember having a similar cough back then, but I didn’t really go to the doctor or anything. It just sort of went away on its own after a while. I guess I thought it would do the same this time, but it’s been longer than I expected, you know?

doctor_turn: Do you have any known allergies to medications, foods, or environmental factors?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any known allergies, really. I haven’t had any issues with medications or foods, and I’m not aware of any environmental triggers either. So, I guess that’s good?

doctor_turn: Can you tell me about any medical conditions in your family?
patient_turn: Sure, um, my mom has diabetes, and my dad has hypertension, but that’s about it. There aren’t any cancers or lung or heart conditions in my family that I know of. So, it seems pretty normal, I guess?

doctor_turn: Have you traveled recently or been in contact with anyone who was sick?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled recently, and I don’t think I’ve been around anyone who was sick either. I’ve just been, um, at home mostly, you know, with my boyfriend. So, I’m not sure where this cough came from.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any environmental triggers in your apartment, like mold or strong odors?
patient_turn: Um, not really, no. I mean, we live in an old apartment, but I haven’t noticed any mold or strong odors that would trigger anything. It seems pretty normal, I guess, but I’m not totally sure.

doctor_turn: Are you up to date with your influenza vaccine for this year?
patient_turn: Oh, um, actually, I’m not up to date with this year’s influenza vaccine. I just haven’t gotten around to it yet, you know? I’ve been meaning to, but it slipped my mind.
